title: UC0 — Install kagent
description: Run this first. Install kagent on your slice so the diagnostic UCs that follow have a working agent runtime.

steps.title: Check your cluster
steps.explanation:
Before installing kagent, confirm your slice is reachable.

- `kubectl config current-context` should show your participant slice.
- `kubectl get nodes` should list at least one node in `Ready` state. If nothing comes back, raise it with the workshop crew before continuing.

steps.title: Install kagent via CLI
steps.explanation:
kagent ships a CLI that handles the full install — CRDs, controller, and the demo profile's pre-packaged agents (the Prometheus / Grafana agents UC3 will reuse).

- `--profile demo` provisions the demo profile so UC3 finds the pre-packaged Prom/Grafana agents already wired (architecture L300). Switch to `--profile minimal` only if UC3's needs change.
- The CLI installs into the `kagent` namespace by default.
- `kubectl rollout status` waits for the controller to be `Available` before we move on.

*Beat 3 install form (frozen by STORY-033 spike): `kagent install --profile demo` — the CLI's only documented profiles are `minimal` and `demo` per `kagent install --help`; `demo` is selected for UC3 reuse.*

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.explanation:
Confirm the kagent CRDs are registered, the controller is `Running`, and any default ModelConfig that ships with the demo profile is visible.

The per-UC `artemis-llm` ModelConfig is created later by UC1's apply step — zero or one ModelConfig in the `kagent` namespace at this point is fine.
